findings.message: Hardcoded secret committed in source: `api_secret_key` defaults to the literal string "super-secret-key-do-not-share". Anyone with repo read access (or a leak of source) gets the secret; this also defeats per-environment rotation and the class docstring's own contract ("All configuration, sourced from environment / .env. Never hardcode config."). If this value is ever used to sign tokens or authenticate callers, the system is trivially bypassable.
findings.suggestion: Do not default a secret in code. Either omit the default and let pydantic-settings require APP_API_SECRET_KEY from the environment/.env (raising on startup if unset), or type it as `SecretStr` with no default and load via the env. Rotate the leaked value immediately and add a pre-commit / CI scan (e.g. gitleaks/trufflehog) so this class of regression is caught at the gate.
